entry.title: Principle of Reorder Point Planning
entry.content:
The basis of reorder point planning is the comparison of the available MRP stock
(total from plant stock and the fixed receipts) with the reorder point. If the available
stock is less than the reorder point, then procurement is triggered.

Reorder Point Planning

The reorder point should cover the expected average material requirements during
the replenishment lead time.

The safety stock should cover both excess material consumption during the
replenishment lead time and the additional consumption caused by delayed deliveries.

The safety stock is therefore part of the reorder point.

The reorder point is composed of the expected average material requirements during
the replenishment lead time and the safety stock. You must therefore consider the
following points when defining the reorder point:

• Safety stock
• Previous consumption or future requirements
• Replenishment lead time

During automatic reorder point planning, you determine the reorder point and the
safety stock in the material master. During automatic reorder point planning, the
integrated forecast program determines the reorder point and safety stock.
The reorder point is calculated as follows: reorder point = safety stock + daily
requirement * lead time.

Inventory management continuously monitors the plant stock during reorder point
planning. At each material withdrawal, the system checks whether the withdrawal
falls short of the reorder point. If this is the case, an entry is created in the planning
file for the next planning run. In the same way, the system checks whether the plant
stock still falls short of the reorder point during a material return. If so, a planning
flag is created so that the planning run can delete superfluous procurement proposals.

If return deliveries become superfluous as a result of fixed scheduled receipts, the
planning run proposes that these receipts are deleted. In this case, the MRP controller
must check with Purchasing whether the purchase order can be retracted.
The system calculates net requirements during the planning run.

entry.title: LSMW with IDOC Method (MATMAS Example)
entry.content:
This outlines using LSMW with the IDOC method to migrate material master data (MATMAS) where IDOCs are the source.

1. Introduction:

  • LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench): A standard SAP tool for data migration from legacy systems or external sources.
  • IDOC (Intermediate Document): A standard SAP format for asynchronous communication between systems. In this case, your external system generates MATMAS IDOCs.

2. Scenario: Migrating Material Master Data (MATMAS) using IDOC as Source:

  • Assumption: An external system creates MATMAS IDOCs containing the material master data to be migrated.

3. LSMW Project Setup:

  • Project: A container for all migration activities. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "MATMAS_MIGRATION").
  • Subproject: A subdivision within a project (e.g., "MATMAS_IDOC_IN").
  • Object: The SAP object to be created/changed (e.g., "0050" for Material Master).

4. Object Attributes:

  • Choose the "IDOC" processing method.

5. IDOC Configuration:

  • Basic Type: Specify the MATMAS IDOC basic type (e.g., MATMAS05). This defines the structure of the IDOC.
  • Message Type: The message type associated with the basic type (e.g., MATMAS).

6. Source Structure:

  • Define the source structure(s) to match the segments of your MATMAS IDOC. Each segment in the IDOC will correspond to a source structure in LSMW. Name these structures descriptively (e.g., "E1MARM", "E1KNMT", etc.). These structures mirror the IDOC segments you'll be receiving.

7. Source Fields:

  • For each source structure, define the fields that correspond to the fields within the IDOC segment. Map these to the fields in the IDOC structure. Pay close attention to data types and lengths.

8. Structure Relations:

  • Define the relationships between the source structures. This mirrors the hierarchical structure of the IDOC. For MATMAS, you'll likely have a main segment (e.g., E1MARM) and dependent segments (e.g., E1KNMT, E1MVKM, etc.).

9. Field Mapping and Conversion Rules:

  • Map the source fields to the target SAP fields. This is where you define how the data from the IDOC will be transferred to the material master.
  • Implement any necessary conversion rules (e.g., data type conversions, value mappings, calculations). ABAP code can be used for complex logic.

10. Create Processing Program:

  • LSMW generates an ABAP program that processes the IDOCs.

11. IDOC File:

  • The IDOCs from your external system need to be available to LSMW. This can be done by storing them in a file on the application server or by using a file port.

12. Read Data:

  • LSMW reads the IDOC data from the file.

13. Convert Data:

  • LSMW converts the data according to your mapping rules.

14. Generate Batch Input Session (or Direct Input/BAPI):

  • LSMW creates a batch input session (or if configured, uses Direct Input or BAPI) to create/update the material master records in SAP. IDOC processing typically uses batch input under the hood.

15. Process Batch Input Session:

  • Run the batch input session to create/update the material master data.

16. Verification:

  • Thoroughly check the created/updated material master records in SAP to ensure the data was migrated correctly. Use standard SAP transactions (e.g., MM03) to verify.

Key Considerations:

  • IDOC Structure: Understanding the MATMAS IDOC structure is crucial.
  • Error Handling: Implement robust error handling to manage any issues during IDOC processing. LSMW provides logging and error analysis tools.
  • Testing: Test the migration thoroughly in a development or test environment before migrating data to production.
  • Performance: For large volumes of data, consider optimizing the LSMW project and using appropriate hardware resources.

This detailed breakdown should provide a comprehensive guide to using LSMW with IDOCs for MATMAS migration. Remember to consult SAP documentation for specific details and best practices.

entry.title: Export and Import tables in SAP from OS level (Control file)
entry.content:
There may be situations where you need to export and import SAP tables from OS level, this can be achieved using R3trans utility in SAP.

Setp 1 :- Collect all the list of tables to be exported.

Step 2 :- Check enough disk space available in the directory where you are going to perform export.

Step 3 :- Create two control files for R3trans which will be used for export & import.

For example :- create control file named as export.ctl and the control file should contain entries like below

export
client=<client no>
file=export_data.dat
select * from <table_name_1>
select * from <table_name_2>
:
:
select * from <table_name_n>


The below are the calling options for R3trans, you can use the below options based on your requirement

 You can specify the following options for calling R3trans:

  • -c <f1> [<f2>] Conversion
    Copies file <f1> to file <f2> by converting the character set to the local character set. If <f2> is omitted, R3trans copies to trans.dat (see Automatic Character Set Conversion with R3trans).
  • -d DB connect
    Check if connect to database is possible and if the ABAP Dictionary is available.
  • -i <file> direct import from data file without specifying a control file (see Import)
  • -l <file> Output of a table of contents to the log file
  • -n Brief information about new features of R3trans not yet documented
  •  -t Test mode All modifications in the database are rolled back.
  •  -u <int> Unconditional mode This option invalidates the rules of the correction and transport system. Each mode can be concatenated (for example -u 12).
  •  -v Verbose mode
    Writes dots to <stdout> and writes additional details to the log file.
  •  -w <file>
    The transport log is written to this file. The default file is trans.log in the current directory.
  •  -x Check whether connection to database can be made

Example:- R3trans export.ctl -w export_log.log

Step 4:- Once export is completed export_data.dat file will get generated.

Step 5:- Use the same .dat file to perform the import, create the import control file (import.ctl) as below

import
client=<client no>
file=export_data.dat


Step 6:- Call R3trans command with the import control file to start import, the table entries will get imported automatically.

Example:- R3trans import.ctl -w import_log.log

Note:- You can even use R3trans to delete a client from OS level, which will remove all the contents of the table with respect to a single client. For that you need to create a control file as below

delete
client=<client no>
select * from 
 
after creating control file you can issue the command as

 Example:- R3trans delete.ctl -w delete_log.log

entry.title: Back Order Processing - Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP in S/4HANA) version 2023 Part II
entry.content:
Advanced Available to Promise (aATP) 2023 – Part 2 

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Available-to-Promise (aATP) is a critical component of the S/4HANA suite that enhances the standard ATP (Available-to-Promise) functionality with more advanced and flexible features. It is designed to meet complex order fulfilment processes and ensure higher accuracy and efficiency in inventory allocation and customer order management.

Backorder Processing (BOP)

What is BOP in S/4HANA

In simple word BOP is nothing but rescheduling of all existing orders with some specific rules with some predefined parameters. BOP was also available in ECC system with less parameters for re-scheduling. In S/4HANA now we have many parameters by which we can reschedule the orders and can allocate, distribute and redistribute the quantities between the sales orders in more efficient way.

We have a scenario where we have committed to fulfilling multiple sales orders based on the quantities and inbound delivery dates outlined in a purchase order. However, the vendor has now informed you of a production delay, and only a partial delivery will be possible.

As a result, you need to update the confirmed delivery dates and quantities for both your sales and inventory orders to reflect these changes. To do all this process in mass BOP will help business in accurate way.

Back-order processing in ECC Vs in S/4HANA

Classical rescheduling selects all requirements for a given material/plant combination.

However, BOP (Backorder Processing) allows for more targeted selection of requirements using various criteria. Additionally, the options for sorting these requirements have been enhanced. Selection and sorting criteria are defined within a "segment." One or more segments are linked to a BOP variant and executed together. The segments linked to a variant can employ one of five different confirmation strategies, which determine the extent to which the requirements should be confirmed.

When multiple segments with different strategies are part of a variant, the confirmation quantities will be higher (and closer to the requested date) based on the priority of the strategy used.

Within aATP, SAP S/4HANA has introduced a new confirmation strategy that categorizes your customers into five different groups:

  • Win: When demand exceeds supply, these customers receive top priority for confirmation and fulfilment.
  • Gain: These customers retain their original confirmations when demand exceeds supply, and additional orders will be confirmed if stock remains after all Win group orders are fulfilled.
  • Redistribute: These customers may lose some of their original commitments to the Win or Gain groups when demand exceeds supply. However, if there is remaining stock after these groups are confirmed, they will be prioritized next.
  • Fill: These customers function similarly to the Redistribute group but won’t receive any additional confirmations.
  • Lose: When demand exceeds supply, these customers will lose their confirmations to higher-priority groups.

BOP set up with Fiori apps -

Let us see how we can setup BOP for sales process in SAP using Fiori apps. Below are the apps which we can use to activate BOP in S/4HANA

  1. Configure BOP segment

This feature enables you to establish sorting guidelines for prioritizing various needs, like sales orders and stock transfers, during a Bill of Process (BOP) execution. You have the flexibility to establish criteria based on factors such as customer importance, delivery deadlines, order categories, Available-to-Promise (ATP) classifications, and custom fields, guaranteeing that the most critical demands are handled at the forefront. This sorting takes place within each individual segment of the BOP.

In the 'Configure BOP Segment' app, you need to maintain the sort attribute and sort sequence. The sort attribute refers to standard parameters such as delivery priority, order type, or document category and so on.

Create segments -

Created DF_BOP_WIN with Selection criteria 1 (High) and Sort attribute with delivery priority and sales document type.

  1. Configure Custom BOP Sorting –

Set Up the BOP Segment Application

This application assists in forming BOP segments by combining like requirements - such as sales orders, stock transfer orders (STOs), and reservations - considering factors like material, plant location, customer, order type, and ATP categories. These segments then allow for focused backorder processing using various BOP strategies such as Win, Gain, Improve, Redistribute, Fill, and Lose. You can also link multiple segments to the same strategy in a specific order.

📌For instance, you might establish one segment for high-priority export orders and another for regular domestic orders, making sure each is handled with the appropriate strategy.

The 'Configure Custom BOP Sorting' app is used to set priorities for specific values. For example, if the sort attribute is set to order type, you must define the priority sequence, such as assigning priority 1 to OR, followed by ZOR, and then YOR.

In the 'Configure BOP Segment' app, you must assign the Sort Sequence you created in the 'Configure Custom BOP Sorting' app.

Users can specify the selection criteria and the sorting sequence for the requirement documents. To the right the specific sorting definition is shown.

 

  1. Configure BOP Variant

As per me BOP variant like a game plan. You get to choose the different BOP strategies you want to use and then match them up with the right BOP segments. Each strategy can have more than one segment linked to it.

When you assign several BOP segments to a single strategy, they will run in the order you set them up. In this part, you will take those BOP segments you set up earlier (in Step 1, where you configured them) and connect them to any of the available strategies, like Win, Gain, Improve, Redistribute, Fill, or even Lose.

The BOP variant acts like a filter, deciding which requirements get included in the process and how they are handled. These set variants are what you will use later on to plan your BOP run. Once everything is set up, the system lets you test out the variant in real-time, and you can even make a copy of it to use with a different strategy.

  1. Schedule BOP Run

Once the BOP variant is created, users can then indicate which variant they'd like to have scheduled. The job can be set to run right away or at a specific time in the future, and users can also set how often it should run. Users have the choice between running a simulation or an actual execution that will update orders.

Users can also turn on the log feature, which creates logs that are then available for viewing within the BOP monitoring application.

 

  1. Monitor BOP Run

Once Job is finised we can Monitor BOP run.

The executed jobs including simulations, can be seen here with details of:

  • Variant name, username, job details and processing status
  • Overall confirmations and details of old confirmation quantity vs. the new confirmation quantity
  • Overall supply assignment quantity and details of old vs. new assignment quantities

📌Let us take live example to check the BOP run result

I created two sales orders, 27 and 28, with quantities of 100 and 50, respectively. Both orders have a customer priority of 2 (Medium).

Currently, there is no stock available, so the quantities remain unconfirmed. To expedite the process, I uploaded 150 units. After running the BOP, the full quantity for both sales orders was successfully confirmed.

After the job is completed, we can track sales order confirmation changes using the 'Monitor BOP Run' app. The system displays both the previous and updated allocations for sales orders, as shown in the example below.

Sales orders 27 and 28 now have fully confirmed quantities, with a delivery priority of 2 (Medium).

Now, let's create a sales order for a different customer with a delivery priority of 1 (High). Upon creation, the system initially confirms zero quantity. The expected behaviour is that the system should redistribute stock from the first two sales orders and allocate it to the new sales order 29 after the BOP run, in line with our WIN strategy.

As shown in the screenshot above, after the BOP run, the system removed the confirmation of 100 units from sales order 27 and 50 units from sales order 28. These released quantities were then allocated to sales order 29, even though it was created later in the system.

Fallback Functionality in SAP Backorder Processing (BOP)

In SAP Advanced ATP (aATP) Backorder Processing (BOP), the fallback functionality ensures that requirements (such as sales orders or stock transfer orders) still receive an allocation if they do not meet the criteria of their initially assigned BOP strategy (Win, Gain, Improve, Redistribute, Fill, Lose).

Example Scenario

- Sales Order A is assigned to the WIN strategy, but due to stock shortages, it cannot be fully confirmed.

- Instead of remaining unconfirmed, the system reassigns it to a fallback strategy like GAIN or REDISTRIBUTE, allowing it to receive at least a partial allocation.

- This helps maintain a balanced and efficient stock allocation process.

entry.title: Routing: Alternative Sequence & Parallel Sequence in SAP
entry.content:
When creating routing in SAP, there are 3 different types of sequences that you can create:

    • Standard Sequence

      A standard sequence is the sequence of operations that describes a production process and from which parallel and alternative sequences can branch. 

      When we create a routing, the system will automatically create a sequence, the standard sequence. The other sequence is either an alternative or a parallel sequence, but they will be connected with the standard routing. Alternative sequences are only for routings, but parallel sequences can be used for routings and rate routings.

    • Alternative Sequence

      An alternative sequence is a sequence of operations that can be used as an alternative to a number of consecutive operations from the standard sequence. This enables you to produce a product in different ways.

      Alternative sequences are used, for example, if the production process differs according to lot size, or if there is a downtime. This is also useful when there is capacity overload, and resource or machine breakdown.

      In creating a new routing sequence, you can go to routing CA01 and choose the Sequences icon.



      Choose the new entry icon to select which sequence you want to create.

      In the sequence details, decide the reference data by entering the branch operation and return operation. 

      Branch operation is from which an alternative sequence is to replace the reference sequence. This is the point in the routing where the system leaves the main sequence and enters an alternative sequence. It serves as the decision point to switch from the standard sequence to an alternative sequence.

       

      Return operation is the operation where the system exits the alternative sequence and returns to the main sequence. After the alternative sequence is completed, production resumes from the return operation, ensuring continuity in the production process.

      Meanwhile the alternative sequence fields are filled with the range of lot size of the production. For Example, the lot sizes are from 200 to 500. So, the system will automatically trigger to use the alternative sequence if the production order lot size is 200 or more until 500.

      • Parallel Sequence

        A parallel sequence enables you to process several operations at the same time and is carried out as the corresponding section in the standard sequence. These are special types of overlapping operations or if several operations are processed simultaneously.  This is the case in the process industry.

        This is useful for minimizing lead times, increasing output, and improving utilization. For example, when we handle complex products, when there is high volume production, also important if there’s no dependency between 2 operations.

        But there are considerations of using parallel sequences which will be complex in planning, monitoring, and resource availability.

        To create a parallel sequence, choose the parallel sequence when creating a new entry.
        In the sequence details, decide the reference data by entering the branch operation and return operation. For example:

        Branch operation is set for operation 0060 which is visual inspection. Then, the Return operation is set for operation 0070 which is labelling. This means all operations from 0060 to 0070 are running at the same time or processed simultaneously.

        So that’s basically the way you create a new routing sequence. This sequence will impact on the production order creation after the MRP runs are created. The difference of the sequence will determine which workstation or resource will be used in the production execution.

        Cheers,
        Dafina

entry.title: LSMW - Legacy System Migration Workbench
entry.content:
LSMW: stands for Legacy System Migration Workbench

The tool is primarily intended for the one-time or periodic transfer of data from external systems (legacy systems) to an SAP system. Thus, it plays a role when switching from third-party systems to SAP products.

The core functions of the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench are:

  • Importing Data from Non-SAP SystemsConversion of data from source format to SAP format.
  • Import of converted data (including consistency check)

Although the name refers to the migration of legacy data, SAP LSMW provides additional functions for creating and changing master data, transaction data, and Customizing data. From a technical point of view, the following options are available:

  • Batch Input: Create (record) and transfer sessions by running transactions.
  • Direct Input: Direct transfer of data to SAP database tables
  • BAPI: Data transfer via interface
  • IDoc: Data Transfer via Standardized Electronic Messages

The data that can be migrated with the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench:

LSMW already enables the transfer of numerous master data and transaction data in the standard system. These include G/L accounts, customer master data, supplier master data, material master data and bills of materials. Of course, it is also possible to transfer documents such as orders, purchase orders or posting documents from financial accounting.

Additional data objects can be migrated by recording transactions if the transaction is suitable for the batch input method. This method can also be used for mass deletion of data.

Application of SAP LSMW with Batch Input:
Put simply, the batch input concept simulates the input of data by a human user. To do this, batch input sessions are first created, which contain both the necessary transaction codes (input screens) and the data to be entered. If the data originates from a non-SAP system, it must first be formatted to conform to the SAP input formats.

Select the Batch Input Recording and click on the overview.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

Click on the "Default All" tab, and the field names will appear at the specific data that was maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button, and the system will display the recording name in the Batch Input Recording under "Define Object Attributes."

By Maintaining the Record name in the Recording field click on the save.

Click on the object Overview and select the table format

The system Picks the field names automatically by recording while creating Material Master.

Application of SAP LSMW with Direct Input:

In contrast to batch input, no sessions are generated with the direct input procedure. The data is updated directly. This means that no screens are run through. Instead, the data is written directly to the target database tables. If errors occur, a restart function can be used. Direct inputs can only be processed in the background to ensure that this is effective.

Direct input is particularly suitable for very large amounts of data, since it offers considerably better performance than batch input. SAP function modules are also available here for consistency checks.

We have to Manually enter the field names in XL Sheet or Notepad.

The Benefits of SAP LSMW are:

  1. The main advantage lies in the considerable reduction of the time and cost involved in data migrations.
  2. Additional simplifications are provided by the reusability of data mapping and conversion rules.
  3. Another positive aspect is that migration can be carried out without programming.
  4. Special knowledge (e.g., ABAP) is only required if very specific steps are to be mapped. Users are guided intuitively through the migration process by a clear sequence of steps.
  5. Another advantage is that LSMW ensures a high level of data quality and consistency through import checks. If the procedure is correct, serious damage to the existing SAP database caused by incorrect imports can be almost ruled out.

Process Flow:

Enter the Transaction Code – LSMW in the Command Field

The Initial screen may appear like this the first step you need to do is Create Project, Sub Project and Object as shown in the screen below:

Maintain the project name and click on Add entry button as shown.

Project can have multiple Subprojects and in turn Subprojects can have multiple Objects as explained below:

By Maintaining the project, subproject and object click on the Tickmark.

Click on the Execute.

In these some fields are optional and some fields will be automatically assigned.

Select the line Define Object Attributes and Click on the Execute.

It will be always in display mode we have to change to change mode manually by clicking on Display <–> Change tab in every step.

Here I’m creating the Materials by using Batch Input Recording.

Select the Batch input Recording and click on the overview. Click on Create Recording.

Here we can maintain the input recording name and description click the enter or click on the Check box which is shown below with the Tick mark.

The system asks to enter the Transaction Code. Enter MM01.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

After saving the material using batch input recording, the initial screen in LSMW will appear as follows: Click on "Default All," and the system will display the field names that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button.

Click on again the back button.

By Maintaining the Record in the field click on the save button.

Some fields will disappear automatically.

Select Define Source structures and click on Execute.

Click on "New Entry" to maintain the source structure and description. Then, click the tick mark and then click on save.

Next, click on "Object Overview" and select the table format.

 

Here, the system displays the fields that can be maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Select the line Define Source fields and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on source structure and click on the Table Maintenance.

Maintain the fields in the source field that correspond to the data entered during the creation of the Material Master.

The fields are assigned to the Source structure by checking the data click on save.

Select the line Define Structure Relations and click on Execute.

The system automatically assigns the source structure to Recording which is created in the batch input Recording.

Select the line Define Field Mapping and Conversion Rules and click on Execute

Place the cursor on the batch recording name, go to "Extras" in the header, and click on "Auto Field Mapping."

Click on "Auto Field Mapping," and the screen will open. Do not change any data; simply click on "Enter."

It will display the fields that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master using the Batch Input recording. Here, simply check the field names and click on the "Accept Proposal" tab.

By checking the data click on the save button.

Select the line Specify Files and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on legacy data and click on Add Entry

Upload the file which is maintained in the Notepad and maintain the name which is mandatory.

  • Select the Tabulator if we maintain data in the Notepad.
  • If We maintained data in XL sheet, select comma tab.

Select the checkbox "Field Names at Start of File" if the field names are maintained in the document that you uploaded in this step.

After specifying the file click on the save button.

Select the line Assign files and click on the Execute.

The system automatically assigns the file to the source Structure. Go to the change mode and click on save.

Select Read Data and Click on Execute button.

Click on the Execute, the system displays a pop up to allow or deny for further steps.

Click on the allow the system picks the data from file that we assigned.

In the file we maintain the data for 3 Materials.

Select Display Read data and click on Execute button.

Click on the Enter or the Tick mark which is shown below.

Select the line, convert data and click on the Execute button.

Click on the Execute.

Select the line Display Converted Data and click on Execute.

Click the Enter button or Tick mark tab which is shown below.

This is the last step if you want to make any changes to the data. Double click on the line item or select the line item and click on the field contents to see the full details.

Select the line Create Batch Input Section and click on the Execute button.

Select the Keep batch input folder check box and click on Execute.

Under 1 batch 3 transactions are created.

Select the line Run Batch Input Session and click on the Execute button

Select the line item and click on Process the system will open the screen.

Process/Foreground:

If we select this indicator, the process will be done in the front end.

Display Errors only:

If we select this indicator, the system directly shows if the data contains any errors.

Background:

If we select this indicator, the whole process will be done in the background.

  • Here I am selecting the Process/Foreground indicator I.e., the process will be the front end.
  • By selecting the required check box (Extended log and Expert mode) click on the process.

Keep Pressing on Enter

After Completing of creation materials, the system shows the pop-up message that Processing of batch input session completed.

Click on the session overview field which is shown below.

The Materials are created successfully.

Conclusion: 

I think this blog will helpful for someone and in summary, LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench) is a crucial tool in SAP for migrating data from legacy systems to SAP. It allows users to efficiently transfer data through a structured process of recording, mapping, and uploading. By automating the data import process, LSMW ensures data accuracy and integrity during migration. Its ease of use and flexibility make it an indispensable tool for successful data migration within SAP systems.

entry.title: LSMW - Legacy System Migration Workbench
entry.content:
LSMW: stands for Legacy System Migration Workbench

The tool is primarily intended for the one-time or periodic transfer of data from external systems (legacy systems) to an SAP system. Thus, it plays a role when switching from third-party systems to SAP products.

The core functions of the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench are:

  • Importing Data from Non-SAP Systems Conversion of data from source format to SAP format.
  • Import of converted data (including consistency check)

Although the name refers to the migration of legacy data, SAP LSMW provides additional functions for creating and changing master data, transaction data, and Customizing data. From a technical point of view, the following options are available:

  • Batch Input: Create (record) and transfer sessions by running transactions.
  • Direct Input: Direct transfer of data to SAP database tables
  • BAPI: Data transfer via interface
  • IDoc: Data Transfer via Standardized Electronic Messages

The data that can be migrated with the SAP Legacy System Migration Workbench:

LSMW already enables the transfer of numerous master data and transaction data in the standard system. These include G/L accounts, customer master data, supplier master data, material master data and bills of materials. Of course, it is also possible to transfer documents such as orders, purchase orders or posting documents from financial accounting.

Additional data objects can be migrated by recording transactions if the transaction is suitable for the batch input method. This method can also be used for mass deletion of data.

Application of SAP LSMW with Batch Input:
Put simply, the batch input concept simulates the input of data by a human user. To do this, batch input sessions are first created, which contain both the necessary transaction codes (input screens) and the data to be entered. If the data originates from a non-SAP system, it must first be formatted to conform to the SAP input formats.

Select the Batch Input Recording and click on the overview.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

Click on the "Default All" tab, and the field names will appear at the specific data that was maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button, and the system will display the recording name in the Batch Input Recording under "Define Object Attributes."

By Maintaining the Record name in the Recording field click on the save.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Click on the object Overview and select the table format

The system Picks the field names automatically by recording while creating Material Master.

 

Application of SAP LSMW with Direct Input:

In contrast to batch input, no sessions are generated with the direct input procedure. The data is updated directly. This means that no screens are run through. Instead, the data is written directly to the target database tables. If errors occur, a restart function can be used. Direct inputs can only be processed in the background to ensure that this is effective.

Direct input is particularly suitable for very large amounts of data, since it offers considerably better performance than batch input. SAP function modules are also available here for consistency checks.

We have to Manually enter the field names in XL Sheet or Notepad.

The Benefits of SAP LSMW are:

  1. The main advantage lies in the considerable reduction of the time and cost involved in data migrations.
  2. Additional simplifications are provided by the reusability of data mapping and conversion rules.
  3. Another positive aspect is that migration can be carried out without programming.
  4. Special knowledge (e.g., ABAP) is only required if very specific steps are to be mapped. Users are guided intuitively through the migration process by a clear sequence of steps.
  5. Another advantage is that LSMW ensures a high level of data quality and consistency through import checks. If the procedure is correct, serious damage to the existing SAP database caused by incorrect imports can be almost ruled out.

 

Process Flow:

Enter the Transaction Code – LSMW in the Command Field

The Initial screen may appear like this the first step you need to do is Create Project, Sub Project and Object as shown in the screen below:

Maintain the project name and click on Add entry button as shown.

Project can have multiple Subprojects and in turn Subprojects can have multiple Objects as explained below:

By Maintaining the project, subproject and object click on the Tickmark.

Click on the Execute.

In these some fields are optional and some fields will be automatically assigned.

Select the line Define Object Attributes and Click on the Execute.

It will be always in display mode we have to change to change mode manually by clicking on Display <–> Change tab in every step.

Here I’m creating the Materials by using Batch Input Recording.

Select the Batch input Recording and click on the overview. Click on Create Recording.

Here we can maintain the input recording name and description click the enter or click on the Check box which is shown below with the Tick mark.

The system asks to enter the Transaction Code. Enter MM01.

The system will take you to the Material Master creation screen and record the data you enter.

After saving the material using batch input recording, the initial screen in LSMW will appear as follows: Click on "Default All," and the system will display the field names that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Click on the back button.

Click on again the back button.

By Maintaining the Record in the field click on the save button.

Some fields will disappear automatically.

Select Define Source structures and click on Execute.

Click on "New Entry" to maintain the source structure and description. Then, click the tick mark and then click on save.

Next, click on "Object Overview" and select the table format.

Here, the system displays the fields that can be maintained during the creation of the Material Master.

Select the line Define Source fields and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on source structure and click on the Table Maintenance.

Maintain the fields in the source field that correspond to the data entered during the creation of the Material Master.

The fields are assigned to the Source structure by checking the data click on save.

Select the line Define Structure Relations and click on Execute.

The system automatically assigns the source structure to Recording which is created in the batch input Recording.

Select the line Define Field Mapping and Conversion Rules and click on Execute

Place the cursor on the batch recording name, go to "Extras" in the header, and click on "Auto Field Mapping."

Click on "Auto Field Mapping," and the screen will open. Do not change any data; simply click on "Enter."

It will display the fields that were maintained during the creation of the Material Master using the Batch Input recording. Here, simply check the field names and click on the "Accept Proposal" tab.

By checking the data click on the save button.

Select the line Specify Files and click on Execute.

Place the cursor on legacy data and click on Add Entry

Upload the file which is maintained in the Notepad and maintain the name which is mandatory.

  • Select the Tabulator if we maintain data in the Notepad.
  • If We maintained data in XL sheet, select comma tab.

Select the checkbox "Field Names at Start of File" if the field names are maintained in the document that you uploaded in this step.

After specifying the file click on the save button.

Select the line Assign files and click on the Execute.

The system automatically assigns the file to the source Structure. Go to the change mode and click on save.

Select Read Data and Click on Execute button.

Click on the Execute, the system displays a pop up to allow or deny for further steps.

Click on the allow the system picks the data from file that we assigned.

In the file we maintain the data for 3 Materials.

Select Display Read data and click on Execute button.

Click on the Enter or the Tick mark which is shown below.

Select the line, convert data and click on the Execute button.

Click on the Execute.

Select the line Display Converted Data and click on Execute.

Click the Enter button or Tick mark tab which is shown below.

This is the last step if you want to make any changes to the data. Double click on the line item or select the line item and click on the field contents to see the full details.

Select the line Create Batch Input Section and click on the Execute button.

Select the Keep batch input folder check box and click on Execute.

Under 1 batch 3 transactions are created.

Select the line Run Batch Input Session and click on the Execute button

Select the line item and click on Process the system will open the screen.

Process/Foreground:

If we select this indicator, the process will be done in the front end.

Display Errors only:

If we select this indicator, the system directly shows if the data contains any errors.

Background:

If we select this indicator, the whole process will be done in the background.

  • Here I am selecting the Process/Foreground indicator I.e., the process will be the front end.
  • By selecting the required check box (Extended log and Expert mode) click on the process.

Keep Pressing on Enter

After Completing of creation materials, the system shows the pop-up message that Processing of batch input session completed.

Click on the session overview field which is shown below.

The Materials are created successfully.

Conclusion: 

I think this blog will helpful for someone and in summary, LSMW (Legacy System Migration Workbench) is a crucial tool in SAP for migrating data from legacy systems to SAP. It allows users to efficiently transfer data through a structured process of recording, mapping, and uploading. By automating the data import process, LSMW ensures data accuracy and integrity during migration. Its ease of use and flexibility make it an indispensable tool for successful data migration within SAP systems.

entry.title: Material Standard Cost Estimate (CO-PC-PCP)
entry.content:
Material cost estimation is a process of calculating the expected cost of manufacturing or procuring material. This is used to determine the standard cost of a product based on its components, production activities, and the overhead costs.

Cost estimation considers several factors such as raw material price, labor costs, machine costs, overhead costs, and subcontracting costs. It ensures accurate product costing, inventory valuation, and profitability analysis. This will be used to determine the pricing of the product and budgeting.

Integration with other module
Integration with other module

Costing materials with a quantity structure enables you to calculate the cost of goods manufactured and cost of goods sold for each product unit without reference to orders. A quantity structure typically consists of a bill of material (BOM) and a routing. But in the process industries, a recipe is used instead of a BOM. You can use the results of material cost estimates with a quantity structure to value materials at standard cost.

The principle of product cost estimation
The principle of product cost estimation

We can cost out individual materials, or multiple materials simultaneously in costing runs. Then, standard costs can be updated to the material master as the future standard price or current standard price.

A. Costing Structure
1. Costing Variant (T-Code: OKKN)
You can define how the cost estimate is calculated by customizing the cost control which contains costing type, valuation variant, and date control.


Maintain costing variant

  • Costing Type: Determines the purpose of the cost estimate (e.g., standard cost estimate, simulation, product cost collector, etc).
  • Valuation Variant: Determines whether standard price or moving average price is used. It also specifies how materials, activity types, and overheads are valued.
  • Quantity structure (BOM & Routing): BOM is to define raw materials and semi-finished products used as the component to produce a product. Routing is to define the operations, work centers, and activities for production. 

    B. Steps in Material Cost Estimate with Quantity Structure
    These are the end-to-end process flow from creating material to material price analysis. But in this article we will just focus on the material cost estimate calculation part.


    Material cost estimation flow in SAP

    1. Go to transaction code CK11N

    Here you need to fill the material and plant. For the costing data, choose the costing variant you want to use as you’ve been configuring it in T-Code OKKN before.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure initial screen

    The dates need to be in the period of the cost center you maintain when maintaining the activity price at T-Code KP26.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure initial screen (Dates)

    2. Make sure all cost structure error status is green. Usually there will be an error message if the calculation is not complete. For example you didn’t fill the cost center planning in KP26. Or you enter the wrong period of pricing so the cost will not be calculated. 

    On the cost component view, you can see the COGS and COGM has been calculated based on production routing, bill of material, and other expenses.


    Material cost estimate with quantity structure

    3. After you get the cost estimate, go to transaction code CK24 to mark and release the standard price. Insert the posting period and make sure the quantity that cost estimate updated successfully is not 0.


    Mark standard price

    Then, you can check the price at material master (T-Code: MM02/MM03 → Costing 2).
    The marked price will appear as the future price, but you can also still see the current price before we run the material cost estimate.


    Material master: Costing 2 Future Price

    If you are sure with the standard price calculation, you can go back to CK24 to release the standard price.


    Release standard price

    You can check back on the material master, the material cost estimate is moved to the current price after we release it.


    Material master: Costing 2 Current Price

     

    So that's basically how you calculate the standard cost from material cost estimates. Feel free to discuss more if you have any other insights or questions! Hope this helps to understand the basics of material cost estimation. 

     

    A lifetime learner,
    Dafina

entry.title: How to Release PR document from HOLD or PARK status using BAPI
entry.content:
Introduction:

A purchase requisition is an internal document used in an organization to request the procurement of goods or services. In SAP, efficient management of purchase requisitions is crucial for smooth procurement operations. This blog will provide a clear guide on releasing PR documents from HOLD or PARK status using BAPIs and releasing the document for further processing.

 

Understanding Parked and Hold Documents in SAP:

First, let's understand the PARK and HOLD options in SAP. These functionalities are essential for managing purchasing documents, allowing users to save them temporarily for later processing.

Hold: If the document has incorrect or incomplete data and you want to save it temporarily to process later, use the Hold option.

Park: If the document has complete and correct data but needs further workflow approvals or budget checks, use the Park functionality.

 

Activating Hold and Park options:

To activate the PARK and HOLD options in transactions ME51N, ME52N, and ME53N, follow these two steps:

  1. Activate Business Functionality
    • Go to T-Code SPRO.
    • Click on Activate Business Functions

       

    • Expand the folder Enterprise_Business_Functions and activate the functionality LOG_MM_CI_3.

       

     2. Enable PARK and HOLD Features In T-Code SPRO, follow this path:
          Materials Management --> Purchasing --> Environment Data --> Activate 'Park and Hold' in Purchasing Documents. 

 By completing these steps, you will enable the PARK and HOLD functionalities for purchasing documents in your SAP system.

 

Changing PR document status from HOLD or PARKED to SAVED using BAPI:

To change the status of a purchase requisition document from HOLD or PARKED to SAVED, you can use the BAPI “BAPI_PR_CHANGE.”. 

Below is an example of how to implement this in ABAP:

 

 

DATA: ls_prheader  TYPE bapimereqheader,
      ls_prheaderx TYPE bapimereqheaderx,
      lt_return    TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2.

IF  ls_prheader-preq_no IS NOT INITIAL.

 ls_prheader = VALUE #( preq_no       = |{ ls_prheader-preq_no ALPHA = IN }|
                        park_complete = abap_false
                        hold_complete = abap_false
                        memorytype    = abap_false ).
  
 ls_prheaderx = VALUE #( preq_no       = abap_true
                         park_complete = abap_true
                         hold_complete = abap_true
                         memorytype    = abap_true ).

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_PR_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
   number     = ls_prheader-preq_no “Your PR Number
    prheader  = ls_prheader
    prheaderx = ls_prheaderx
  TABLES
    return   = lt_return.
IF VALUE #( lt_return[ type = 'S' ] OPTIONAL ) IS NOT INITIAL.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
       wait = abap_true.
ELSE.
    ROLLBACK WORK.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

Release Strategy:

The release strategy in SAP defines the approval process for purchase requisitions:

  • Purpose: Ensures PRs are reviewed and approved before conversion to purchase orders.
  • Release Codes: Each strategy can have up to eight release codes, indicating who must approve the requisition.
  • Approval Sequence: Specifies the order in which approvals must be obtained.
  • Assignment: Release strategies are assigned based on release conditions like document type and value.

 

Release Code:

A release code is a two-character ID that authorizes a person to approve or release a purchase requisition or external purchasing document.

  • Purpose: It serves as an electronic signature, replacing traditional handwritten approvals. This digitization streamlines the approval process and enhances efficiency.
  • Assignment: Release codes are assigned to employees based on their roles and authorization levels within the organization. For example, a clerk might have a release code for initial approvals, while a manager has a higher-level code.
  • Configuration:
      • You can define up to eight release codes within a release strategy.
      • Each code is linked to specific approval levels, ensuring that the correct personnel review PRs based on their value or other criteria.
  • Approval Process: The release strategy specifies which release codes must be used and the sequence of approvals required.

 

Releasing PR documents using BAPI:

To release a PR that needs approval from respective employees/approvers in your organization, you can use the BAPI - “BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE_GEN”.

Below is an example implementation:

 

 

 

DATA : lt_return TYPE TABLE OF bapireturn.
 
 CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_REQUISITION_RELEASE_GEN'
   EXPORTING
     number     = iv_pr_num       “Your PR Number
     rel_code   = iv_release_code “Release code
   TABLES
     return      = lt_return.
 IF VALUE #( lt_return[ type = 'S' ] OPTIONAL ) IS NOT INITIAL OR 
    lt_return IS INITIAL.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
       wait = abap_true.
 ELSE.
    ROLLBACK WORK.
 ENDIF.

 

 

 

 

Finding Release Codes:

You can find release codes under the Release Strategy tab in transactions ME51N, ME52N, and ME53N.

The following tables are useful for retrieving relevant information:

  • EBAN: To get the release group and release strategy for the PR number.
  • T16FS: To get release codes for a particular release group & strategy.
  • T16FW: To get approver names/IDs for a release code.

 

Conclusion:

This blog emphasized the significance of parked and hold documents, the activation of essential functionalities, and the use of BAPIs to change PR statuses. We also explored the release procedure and strategy, illustrating how structured approvals can enhance efficiency in your procurement processes.

Thank you for reading!

entry.title: SSL Certificate Expiry Notification of 3rd party systems in STRUST
entry.content:
We can set up the SSL (HTTPS) certificate installed in SAP STRUST expirty notifications, which will help avoid connection losses in production systems. 

Pre requisites :

1. create one Batch user - CERTEXPIRE with the below Roles.  

Object: S_RFC
- RFC_TYPE: FUGR (function group)
- RFC_NAME: SALRT_WPRFC
- ACTVT: 16 (execute)

2. Activate the below service

 Default_host  sap bc  bsp  sap alert inbox  , and Activate in the context menu.

  1. assign the SAP Basis DL (XXXX@companydomain.com ) to user CERTEXPIRE in SU01
  2. assign the User – CERTEXPIRE in ALRTCATDEF
  • Call transaction ALRTCATDEF
  • Go to the menu "Settings > Configuration"
  • Under the section "Inbound Processing", enter a user name that exist in user management (transaction SU01)
  1. can you check SALRT1 and  the alert RFC server group as NONE
  2. update the Sap Basis DL in SCOT

6 .  Call transaction - ALRTCATDEF -> Security-Relevant Alerts ->

Message Title - &SYST-SYSID& Certificate Expiry Notifications

Short Text

  • Certificate expired in &DAYS& Days in system &SYS& (PSE Type > &PSE&)

Long Text

  • The system "&SYS&" determined that a Certificate expired in "&DAYS&" Days in system &SYS& (PSE Type > &PSE&)

You must extend or renew this certificate immediately.

Run the report SSF_ALERT_CERTEXPIRE. 

This report produces a list of installed certificates and their expiration dates.

Alternatively, call transaction STRUST. The message displayed contains the PSE type (a node) in which you can find the certificate in question.

Reference Notes :

  1. 1690014 - Alerts can not be sent via mail through Alert Framework
  2. 1504005 - Error SALERT007 "Alert has no recipients" using Alert Management Framework
  3. https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/si/3362959386.html
  4. 3008997 - SSF_ALERT_CERTEXPIRE does not send email alert
  5. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ssf-alert-certexpire-invalid-message-received-in-email/ba-p/13202814
  6. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_701/6f50deaa6c4b1014aa61d2ce22076cab/494782ba37042221e10000000a42189d.html?version=7.01.19
  7. 934993 - CCMS settings missing from Alert Management (ALM)
  8. 3335612 - SSF_ALERT_CERTEXPIRE send email alert with "incorrect" time slot

entry.title: Functional Highlights of the New 3.0 FP02 Release of PLM System Integration for SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear PLM community,

two months into the new year, we are happy to offer you a new feature pack of PLM System Integration for SAP S/4HANA. Our engineering teams did their best to deliver a wealth of new features and improvements that can help you enhance system integration and streamline your business processes.

As part of this 3.0 FP02 release, you can use a new Domain Model entity to create Manufacturing Reference Structures, leverage new integration capabilities for SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for extended production engineering and operations (PEO), enjoy improved list handling for production operations, and use additional material master views. Furthermore, our new BAdIs, custom logic capabilities, and data cleanup processes can help you achieve greater efficiency and effectiveness in your SAP operations. Read on to find out all the details that will help you make the most of the new features. 

 

Create Manufacturing Reference Structures

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA now supports the creation of basic manufacturing reference structures via a new Domain Model entity View Structure Version (VSV). You can transfer source structures from an external product lifecycle management (PLM) system and use them for manufacturing processes. The integration can create SAP PEO MRS objects, MRS structure items, and BOM links.

 

Realize Manufacturing Process Definition Scenarios for SAP PEO

PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA now allows you to set up a manufacturing process definition scenario for SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for production engineering and operations: Transfer variable production operation lists for configurable routings that support logical component allocations and provide the manufacturing engineering department with all the information they need.

Transfer Work Instructions From an External PLM system

You can also use our integration to transfer work instructions that were authored in an external product lifecycle management (PLM) system to the production integration portal of SAP S/4HANA Manufacturing for extended production engineering and operations. This will help you improve your master data quality as well as transparency across different domains by sharing information between the engineering department and the extended supply chain.

 

Enjoy Improved List Handling in Production Operations

Leverage advanced list handling capabilities for production operations as part of the routing transfer, such as parallel operation constellation and other effectivity information. Enable the parallelization of sequences for classic routing scenarios. Based on a POL, you can create a classic PP routing in the SAP system, supporting a default mode with only a standard sequence with one predecessor per operation. For more advanced routing, a sequence-based mode allows you to use standard, parallel, and alternative sequences, while a heuristic-based mode automatically determines standard and parallel sequences based on operation predecessors.

 

 Profit from Additional Material Master Views

Extend the creation of material master data beyond basic and plant-specific views. With this new release, you can create views such as default sales or quality management views, from scratch or based on specific view templates. Leveraging BRF+, these views help you to improve master data quality and enhance communication between engineering and the extended supply chain.

 

What Else is New in PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA?

Besides the innovations above, we further improved your trusted PLMSI add-on to deliver you a range of new functions. Enjoy!

Enhanced Integration Capabilities thanks to New BAdIs and Custom Logic

We have introduced several new Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) that provide greater flexibility and customization options. Use additional extension fields, optimize your customer order item processing, and leverage new methods for defining custom logic that allow for more precise handling of inbound and outbound requests. For detailed information, see our What's New Viewer.

Streamlined Data Cleanup Processes

The supported data cleanup processes have been streamlined for better usability. Access a comprehensive group of data cleanup functions from just one report, including the deletion of obsolete migration data, selected key mappings or PLMSI messages and logs, and the cleaning up orphaned key mappings. This consolidation simplifies the data cleanup process and ensures that all relevant tasks are performed efficiently.

 

Improved Usability of the PLMSI Workbench

The usability of the PLMSI Workbench has been significantly enhanced with new functions. Users can now edit requests of messages that have not been processed directly on the Request tab, call up aggregated logs for selected tasks and subtasks, and navigate to the source code of an object directly from the object list of a processing step. Furthermore, you can navigate directly to PLM system integration Customizing or initiate data cleanup.

 

 

Business Content

As for each feature pack, we have updated our comprehensive set of business content (BC) sets to help you configure your system with ease. For details, see Initial Configuration.

For a comprehensive list of our new and enhanced features for release 3.0 FP02, check out our What's New Viewer.

Expand Your Range

With our past release, we had expanded the range of external PLM systems you can connect to your SAP system using PLM system integration. With this release, we proudly announce that our partner product PLM system integration, add-on for 3DEXPERIENCE has already published its second release, 3.1. Check out the Release Notes to learn about latest updates.

All relevant information on PLM system integration is also available via the links below.

  • SAP Help Portal
    • Documentation of PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA

 SAP Product Availability Matrix (SAP login required):

    • Matrix for PLM system integration 3.0 for SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Roadmap Explorer:
    • SAP Roadmap for PLM system integration

And last but not least – contact us via our excellent forum if you have any questions.

entry.title: Deactivating Simplified Sourcing in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA introduced several simplifications in Production Planning, especially for Material Requirements Planning (MRP), as I explained in my blog MRP: Simplifications in S/4HANA. One of these simplifications is known as Simplified Sourcing, and it changes the logic used by MRP to determine the source of supply for a replenishment proposal with external procurement.

Some of the changes introduced by Simplified Sourcing in the MRP source determination were:

  • Source List is no longer mandatory for source determination.
  • Checking the new flag in “Auto Sourcing” in the info record is enough to make a source of supply relevant for MRP.
  • Contracts and Scheduling Agreements are automatically selected by MRP as a source of supply, if they exist.

SAP note 2268069 explains the simplified sourcing logic in detail, and it suggests that it is simpler than classic MRP because of the following reasons:

  • The different sources of supply are pretty symmetrical. Sources of supply have a temporal validity, a quantitative validity, a deletion indicator, and a "relevant for automatic sourcing" indicator. The sourcing logic can treat the different sources of supply in a uniform way
  • Quota arrangements can be used to prioritize any of the possible sources of supply
  • Purchasing info records are proper sources of supply. The SAP S/4HANA MRP can select purchasing info records without source list entries. It is sufficient to define the supplier information of a material in the purchasing info record.
  • Production versions make sure BOM and routing fit together
  • Production versions have always been the only in-house production source of supply integrated with APO PP/DS. With production versions as the only in-house production source of supply, ERP sourcing and PP/DS sourcing become more similar.
  • Purchasing info records, contracts, and delivery schedules have always been the external procurement sources of supply integrated with APO. With purchasing info records being proper sources of supply, ERP sourcing and PP/DS sourcing become more similar
  • A unified source of supply model is the basis for future innovations in sourcing

 

While simplified sourcing indeed streamlines the master data required for source determination, it brings one additional challenge to companies that are migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA, as they will need to review the existing master data, in order to make sure that source determination works in SAP S/4HANA.

In order to minimize the migration efforts, SAP implemented a new setting that allows simplified sourcing to be deactivated for external procurement. This new logic can be introduced into all the supported SAP S/4HANA releases with the following notes:

3552667 - Feature: Disable/Enable simplified sourcing in S/4HANA for plants - Dynpro changes
3493681 - Feature: Disable/Enable simplified sourcing in S/4HANA for plants.

SAP Note 3493681 introduces the new flag 'SKIP_SIMPLIFIED_SOURCING' in table T399D, and when this flag is checked, then only the source list is considered by both MRP and MRP Live as a valid source of supply.

SAP Note 3552667 implements the screen changes and it brings the field 'Skip Simplified Sourcing' into customizing. This flag can be accessed either through customizing transaction OMDT (external procurement customizing) or OPPQ (plant parameters customizing). Figure 1 shows the setting checked in view Default Values for Purchasing/MRP.

Figure 1: Skip Simplified Sourcing

 

 

Now let’s see some examples of how exactly this flag works in MRP and observe the MRP behavior with different combination of settings.

Example 1:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Unchecked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record
  • No Source List

In this example we will just simulate the standard Simplified Sourcing logic, because the new flag Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked. Figure 2 shows that the flag Auto Sourcing is checked in the Info Record of supplier VENDOR_001 for material S4HANA_ROH_005.

Figure 2: Auto Sourcing checked in the Info Record

 

After running MRP, we can see in transaction that this supplier has been selected as a valid source of supply for a purchase requisition created by MRP, because the Simplified Sourcing logic was used by MRP.

Figure 3: Source of Supply determined by MRP

 

Now let’s go to the next example, where we will deactivate Simplified Sourcing and run MRP again, with the same master data.

Example 1:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record
  • No Source List

In the new example we kept the same master data previously used, but we have checked the flag Skip Simplified Sourcing in the MRP customizing.

Figure 4: Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked in customizing

 

When we run MRP for the same material and with an identical planning situation, we can observe that MRP could not determine a vendor for the purchase requisition. That happened because we don’t have a source list for our material, and with the Simplified Sourcing logic deactivated, the Source List becomes mandatory for the MRP Source Determination.

Note that the system behavior will be the same if we run Classic MRP  or MRP Live.

 

Figure 5: Source of Supply was not determined by MRP, because there is no Source List

 

Now let’s see another example, where we will keep Simplified Sourcing deactivated and we will create a Source List.

Example 3:

  • Flag Skip Simplified Sourcing Checked
  • Auto Sourcing Checked in the Info Record for VENDOR_001
  • Source List created where VENDOR_002 is relevant to MRP.

In this third example we will keep the Skip Simplified Sourcing flag checked, and we will create a Source List, but the supplier relevant to MRP will be VENDOR_002, for which the Auto Sourcing flag is unchecked in the Info Record.

Figure 6: Source List with a valid Source of Supply

 

After we run MRP for our material, we can observe in MD04 that VENDOR_002 has been selected as the purchase requisition source of supply, as it is relevant for MRP in the source list.

Figure 7: Source of Supply determined by MRP

 

 

In the examples above we have demonstrated the source determination logic using only Info Records and Source Lists, but the results would be similar when using a Contract or Scheduling agreement:

  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is checked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will be automatically selected as a source of supply.
  • If the Skip Simplified Sourcing is unchecked, Contracts and Scheduling Agreements will only be selected as a source of supply if there is a source list entry determining that they should be selected by MRP.

 

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG

entry.title: Do you know how you can leverage SAP LeanIX to run an Application Rationalization?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Mastering your ERP Transformation with SAP LeanIX learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will enable you to take your ERP transformation expertise to the next level with related advanced training session. Designed for Enterprise Architects and IT professionals, this Learning Journey focuses on aligning processes, applications, data, and people to ensure a smooth and successful transformation. 

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Gain hands-on experience in scoping, planning, and visualizing ERP transformation initiatives with SAP LeanIX.
  • Learn best practices for managing complex ERP landscapes and aligning IT with business strategy.
  • Engage in peer discussions to exchange insights, challenges, and solutions on key transformation topics.

Prerequisites

  • Mastering the Application Rationalization with SAP LeanIX

Practice your skills in a live SAP environment

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Customer Vendor Integration - Contact persons number range
entry.content:
Requirement

Customer and Vendor Integration (CVI) for system conversion to SAP S/4HANA is detailed out in KBA 2713963. With ref to this, by customization SAP offers only internal number range for creation of contact persons. For creating contact persons in external number range, the only option we have is to go for an enhancement. This blog details out the steps that you can follow in order to achieve this.

Business scenario:

I came across a customer which has SAP CRM as a leading system for customer master and SAP ECC is receiving customers and contact persons via SAP CRM middleware. While preparing for preparing for ECC system conversion to S/4HANA, after maintaining all CVI customization, when we ran some synchronisation tests for customers, we learnt that the business partner

  1. corresponding to a customer in ECC is created with same GUID and partner number as in CRM
  2. corresponding to a contact person in ECC is created with same GUID but with different partner number as in CRM

The requirement here was to the get contact person created as business partner in ECC with same number as in CRM. The solution detailed below can be used for any business scenario where you need CVI contact person business partner to have external number range.

Ensure that

1. All configuration for CVI is maintained

2. The customer synchronization is not started yet. Any contact person already created (out of customer synchronisation) with internal number can not be changed.

Solution

1. Define external number range

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings - > Define Number Ranges

2. Define a grouping and assign external number range to it

IMG -> Cross-Application Components -> SAP Business Partner -> Business Partner -> Basic Settings -> Number Ranges and Groupings - > Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges

3. Create enhancement implementation of enhancement spot CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER

Implement method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUST_CONT_TO_BP_AND_REL; this method allows us to enhance contact person data assignment to business partner data

Sample code

Method IF_EX_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER~MAP_CUST_CONT_TO_BP_AND_REL of BADI implementation ZCL_CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER in ECC

 

 

 

 

  METHOD if_ex_cvi_custom_mapper~map_cust_cont_to_bp_and_rel.

    DATA: lv_partner_guid TYPE bu_partner_guid,
          lv_partner_no   TYPE bu_partner.

* Convert GUID from CHAR32 to RAW16
    lv_partner_guid    = c_person-header-object_instance-bpartnerguid.

* Fetch contact person ID from SAP CRM (Write your own logic to determine contact person ID)
    CALL FUNCTION 'ZGET_BP_NUMBER_FROM_GUID'
      DESTINATION '<remote destination of SAP CRM>'
      EXPORTING
        iv_guid               = lv_partner_guid
      IMPORTING
        ev_number             = lv_partner_no
      EXCEPTIONS
        system_failure        = 1
        communication_failure = 2
        OTHERS                = 3.

* Change contact person grouping and assign an external number (only when a unique number is determined)
    IF sy-subrc EQ 0 AND lv_partner_no IS NOT INITIAL.
      c_person-central_data-common-data-bp_control-grouping = 'ZCPE'.
      c_person-header-object_instance-bpartner = lv_partner_no.
    ENDIF.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

 

 

Remote-enabled function module ZGET_BP_NUMBER_FROM_GUID in CRM

 

 

 

 

FUNCTION zget_bp_number_from_guid.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     VALUE(IV_GUID) TYPE  BU_PARTNER_GUID
*"  EXPORTING
*"     VALUE(EV_NUMBER) TYPE  BU_PARTNER
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------


  SELECT SINGLE partner FROM but000 INTO ev_number WHERE partner_guid EQ iv_guid.


ENDFUNCTION.

entry.title: Stay Certified with SAP Learning
entry.content:
How long is my certification valid?

Because SAP products evolve rapidly, as of April 2024, everyone with an SAP Certification needs to keep their skills and certification valid by completing mandatory yearly assessments.

You need an active SAP Learning Hub subscription to take the short assessment to stay certified. You can access preparation material and take the assessment at any time.

SAP Certifications are valid for one year. If your certification was issued before April 2024, it will expire on March 31, 2025.

Follow these steps to stay certified

  1. Get an SAP Universal ID - Make sure you have a valid SAP Universal ID with at least two email accounts assigned to it.
  2. SAP Learning Hub subscription - Ensure you have an SAP Learning Hub subscription to access the assessment to stay certified. If you have no subscription, you’ll need to book and retake the full 2-3 hour SAP Certification exam every year.
  3. Check your certification status - Log in to My Learning to see your certifications and their expiration dates. Make sure you complete the short annual assessment as soon as possible to extend the validity of your certification by 12 months. If the certification has been retired and you want to retain your certified status, you need to re-certify in an alternative certification. Find recommended alternatives using the SAP Certification finder.
  4. Prepare for the assessment - Find preparation courses or live sessions linked under your certification.
  5. Take the assessment - The assessment is a short, non-proctored quiz with unlimited attempts. Once you’ve completed it successfully, the validity of your certification will be extended by 12 months.
  6. Congratulations - After completing the assessment with the status of approved, click on “Verify course completion”, then it will be confirmed that you have completed the process to remain certified. In my case, 1 day after completing the assessment, the certification renewal date was updated for another 12 months in SAP Learning and Credly.

I hope this blog has helped you renew your SAP certification.

If you found this blog useful, please like, follow and share your knowledge.

Feel free to leave your comment or ask any questions related to the topic.

Clóvis Müller

entry.title: AI Solutions in Global ERP Systems: Accelleron's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation
entry.content:
Accelleron's digital transformation journey with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition was the focus of an episode 116 of the Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud podcast. Hosted by Markus Oertelt, the episode featured insights from Annika Baechle, SAP Application Owner with a focus on sales and services at Accelleron, and Alexander Finger, CTO at SAP Switzerland.

Together, they discussed their experiences in implementing SAP solutions and leveraging artificial intelligence for operational efficiency. This blog post distils their conversation, emphasizing Accelleron's strategic moves and SAP's role in facilitating their journey.

Accelleron's aim to enhance efficiency and sustainability in turbocharger development was a central theme of the discussion. Annika shared a use case highlighting the importance of logistical efficiency in their operations, emphasizing the significant role of turbochargers in reducing emissions while ensuring robust engine performance. The discussion outlined Accelleron's readiness to provide rapid logistical support, ensuring seamless operations for customers worldwide.

Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Faced with the complex task of migrating from ABB’s SAP ERP central component, Accelleron decided to standardize its processes through SAP's offerings. The transition to a dual-tier SAP setup, incorporating both an on-premise ERP system and a global SAP S/4HANA Cloud system, transformed Accelleron's operational approach. This strategic change enabled seamless service integration across 40 countries, aligning processes with SAP's best practices to minimize deviations and ensure compliance with regional regulations.

A significant milestone in this journey was achieving a global rollout of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud in two-and-a-half years. This project involved integrating processes across numerous locations, a task that demanded careful alignment with SAP’s established best practices. The result was enhanced operational coherence and adherence to legal requirements, reinforcing Accelleron's commitment to leveraging technology for operational excellence.

AI Solutions and SAP Switzerland's AI Hackathon

In association with SAP Switzerland's AI Hackathon, Accelleron developed a novel AI-driven tool, the "Test Plan Ninja." This SAP Fiori app was designed to automate the creation of test plans and processes, facilitating efficient regression testing across the company’s extensive network. Despite technical challenges, Accelleron used OpenAI, custom CDS views, and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to streamline test processes. Annika emphasized how this tool simplifies testing, ensuring comprehensive coverage across regions and exemplifying Accelleron's dedication to innovation in quality assurance.

Alexander Finger provided insights into the SAP Switzerland AI Hackathon, highlighting its role in encouraging innovation across different industries. Participants engage in the event to craft use cases that are evaluated based on their originality, integration with existing systems, and business impact. The Hackathon emerged as a dynamic platform fostering creativity and collaboration, with the Test Plan Ninja serving as a successful example of what can emerge from such environments.

Learn more about the SAP’s Switzerland Hackathon

The collaboration between Accelleron and SAP underscores the capacity of technology to drive meaningful advancements in industry processes. Their journey highlights the importance of adopting flexible, scalable solutions like SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which facilitate efficiency and support sustainable practices.

 

Take the chance to listen to the episode 116: AI Solutions in Global ERP Systems – Accelleron's SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Implementation Journey. Click here, tune in and subscribe.

Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success!
